Question: Do you have a repeatable security hardening process? If yes, please explain.

Proposed Answer: Yes. Vulnerability scanning runs against in-scope hosted services (Aptible, AWS) per Information Security Framework §6; dependency vulnerabilities are surfaced via GitHub Dependabot and triaged at least weekly with Critical/High remediated within 30 days and Medium within 90 days per the Framework §6 patch-SLA addition.

Question: Do you protect against attackers compromising passwords, keys, or session tokens? If yes, please explain.
Proposed Answer: Yes. Passwords, keys, and tokens are protected per the Password Management Policy (strong construction, no shared use, deactivation on termination), the Encryption Policy (FIPS 140-2 key custody), and the §III session-token addition (signed short-lived tokens, rate-limited failed logins, session invalidation on logout / password change / role revocation).
Plain English: Do you stop attackers from stealing or guessing user passwords and login session cookies?
Gap Description: Source CSV flagged Remediate=Yes; Password Management Policy currently covers user password hygiene but lacks explicit session-token / brute-force language.
Required Fix - Text: Application sessions shall be protected by signed, short-lived tokens; failed-login attempts shall be rate-limited and monitored; and session invalidation shall occur on logout, password change, or role revocation.
